meaning: n.酵素,发酵,动乱v.(使)发酵,(使)激动,(使)动乱
examples: Foods that ferment quickly in the stomach are excellent natural laxatives.
The dried grapes are allowed to ferment until there is no sugar left and the wine is dry.
Before it even has time to ferment, to quickly offer something to the deity again, to procure favor for the rest of the crop.
Whatever else is going on today, there is no similar intellectual ferment in the air.
The molasses is mixed with water and yeast and is left to ferment (beer).
